title: Tapping for Mum Guilt
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the guilt of never feeling like quite enough
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the mum guilt that runs as a background track: the sense of never quite measuring up, of always being too short on patience or time or attention. Works through the specific guilt and toward a place where being a good-enough mother is a real and sufficient thing.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s). The mum guilt framing draws on common patterns in matrescence writing and maternal psychology.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the guilt of not being enough as a mother is loud, or after a day where you have snapped, missed something, or been absent in some way.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for mum guilt. The script works through the specific guilt of not measuring up to an internal standard of what a good mother looks like, and toward a place where the effort of mothering is acknowledged and the standard is brought into contact with reality.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I don't feel like I am doing this well enough, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the guilt is almost constant and I can't quite switch it off, I am open to releasing some of this weight.
Even though I hold myself to a standard no one else would hold me to, I accept that I am doing my best with what I have.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: I snapped today and I can't stop thinking about it.
Side of Eye: The standard I am holding myself to is impossible to meet.
Under Eye: The guilt that runs even on the good days.
Under Nose: Feeling like I am always one wrong move away from failing them.
Chin: How heavy it is to carry this much responsibility.
Collarbone: The gap between the mother I want to be and the one I feel I am.
Under Arm: I love them and it still isn't enough, apparently.
Top of Head: The guilt of being human when they need something more.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I showed up for them today. That counts.
Side of Eye: The standard I hold myself to is not the standard they need from me.
Under Eye: Good enough is a real and sufficient thing.
Under Nose: They see someone who loves them. That is the main thing.
Chin: I can repair when I get it wrong. That is what they learn from.
Collarbone: I am allowed to be imperfect and still be a good mother.
Under Arm: Guilt that won't ease is not information about my quality. It is a pattern to release.
Top of Head: I am doing this. It counts.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
